message:
feat(btree): free child subtree pages when collapsing empty interior root to leaf

When an interior root page has zero divider cells (all rows deleted), the
collapse_empty_table_root_if_needed path rewrites the root as an empty leaf.
Previously, the child pages (left children of former divider cells and the
right-child pointer) were orphaned — they remained allocated in the freelist
bitmap but were never returned to the free page pool, causing a slow page
leak under repeated DELETE-all / reinsert workloads.

This commit adds a recursive free_subtree_pages helper that walks the
interior→leaf spine depth-first and calls pager.free_page on every
descendant before the root is overwritten. The traversal is bounded by tree
depth (≤ ~20 for any practical database) so stack usage is negligible.

Changes:
- Add BtCursor::free_subtree_pages(cx, page_no) — recursive DFS that
  parses each page header, follows left-child / right-child pointers on
  interior pages, and frees leaf pages on the way back up
- Call free_subtree_pages for every child referenced by the empty interior
  root before zeroing it into a leaf in collapse_empty_table_root_if_needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
